title: gnaphalium canescens subsp. microcephalum
intro: Gnaphalium canescens subsp. microcephalum, also known as Microcephalus White cudweed, is a subspecies of the Gnaphalium canescens plant, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Gnaphalium canescens subsp. microcephalum include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications, such as creams and salves, for its medicinal and therapeutic benefits.
